issue_title: Add Unit Tests for Lpar class
Room class constructor doesn't produce a valid Room object
issue_body:
Hi all,

I'm trying to take a serialized Room object and recreate a Room object, by passing the deserialized room map to the constructor. I seem to get a well valid Room object, but I can't use this object to actually send notifications.

Version:
`hypchat==0.18`

Example code:

```
# hipchat_handler encapsulates a hypchat client, omitting details

>>> hipchat = hipchat_handler.hipchat
>>> type(hipchat)
<class 'hypchat.HypChat'>
>>> actual_room = hipchat.get_room('Sample Room')
>>> actual_room.notification('Test')

# Notification succeeds at this point.

>>> import ujson
>>> serialized_room = ujson.dumps(ujson)
>>> from hypchat.restobject import Room
>>> new_room = Room(ujson.loads(serialized_room))
>>> new_room.notification('Test 2')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/var/cache/<SERVICE>/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/hypchat/restobject.py", line 155, in notification
    self._requests.post(self.url+'/notification', data=data)
  File "/var/cache/<SERVICE>/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/hypchat/restobject.py", line 104, in __getattr__
    raise AttributeError("%r object has no attribute %r" % (type(self).__name__, name))
AttributeError: 'Room' object has no attribute '_requests'
```

Reading through the code it seems like `_requests` is set by the Linker only, after the room is created. I can work my current issues by manually setting the `_requests` object in `new_room`, but it tripped up my expectation that the constructor would produce a valid Room object. Maybe the _requests object can be passed into the constructor explicitly, or it can be extracted from some module?

issue_title: SystemError: error return without exception set
issue_body:
@gforcada and I are running into semi-rare weird error messages that seem to indicate that something in the BTrees can't handle issues when an object can't be loaded properly. This seems to be somewhat of a race condition around the C-level implementation of getitem, as many times the exception will occur around something like those lines of code:

```
  File "/srv/s-derfreitag/deployment/work/plone/eggs/zope.intid-3.7.2-py2.7.egg/zope/intid/__init__.py", line 87, in getId
    return self.ids[key]
SystemError: error return without exception set
```

```
  Module Products.PluginIndexes.common.UnIndex, line 458, in _apply_index
SystemError: error return without exception set
```

```
    return self._tree[id].__of__(self)
SystemError: error return without exception set
```

Whenever this appears, either the Zope server is being shut down at the moment and ZEO has already disconnected, or the ZEO server has had a disconnect and immediately before those errors we see the ZEO client complaining:

```
  File "/srv/s-derfreitag/deployment/work/plone/eggs/ZODB3-3.10.7-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg/ZODB/Connection.py", line 901, in _setstate
    p, serial = self._storage.load(obj._p_oid, '')
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'load'
```

```
  File "/srv/s-derfreitag/deployment/work/plone/eggs/ZODB3-3.10.7-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg/ZEO/ClientStorage.py", line 88, in __getattr__
    raise ClientDisconnected()
```

One thing I'm wondering about is why there would be anything still processing data or requests if Zope already disconnected the database. My memory tells me that Zope would allow threads to finish their requests and then shut down. 

So, from reading and googling the error message it seems somewhere the Python C API is signalled an error but no exception is set. I looked around a bit and a wild guess would be that this could be an issue from cPersistence in the PER_USE macro (it seems to signal an error condition but doesn't set an exception apparently) or by the _bucket_get function in BucketTemplate.c:86 where we return NULL indicating an error and seem to implictly rely on someone else already having set an exception. I could be digging at the completely wrong end, though ... :)

issue_title: Fix tag filtering in image lists
issue_body: Tag filtering is being ignored.  Fix it.  Part of #164 - not really related to horizon as much as using glance's API correctly.

This issue is based on a conversation with Matt George (Berkeley), who is using GalSim to simulate some profiles that would be observed with a spectroscopic fiber placed on a part of a galaxy:

It would be useful to have a method like getApertureFlux() that would let you take a GSObject and a specification of the aperture (circular or elliptical with some configuration), and say how much flux is in that aperture.

This is low priority compared to issues required for GREAT3, but I thought it was a neat idea to do at some point.

issue_title: Use less RAM Integrating chromatic images
issue_body: @sowmyakth has been running into some RAM restrictions using ChromaticRealGalaxy on the SLAC clusters.  We identified at least one piece of galsim that could be improved in this respect, which is the way integ.py does the wavelength integral for chromatic drawImage and drawKImage.  Currently, the monochromatic images in the integral are first all drawn and put in memory, and then added together (with weights) at the end.  I think it's straight-forward to instead accumulate the integral as monochromatic images are drawn though, which would would have saved, in this particular case, around 1.5 GB of RAM.

issue_title: Why do we restart pagination from the beginning?
issue_body:
While working on #1576, I saw that occasionally we would generate a pagination token that would not match any records (i.e. it was at the very end of the list of objects to paginate). When this happened, the memory backend would restart pagination at the beginning. However, I can't tell if this behavior is intentional or not and what the reasoning for it is.

One reason it might be intentional: you can hit this behavior if someone inserts elements at the beginning of a list you're in the middle of paginating. In that case, restarting pagination from the beginning is the only way to eventually get to all of the items.

One reason it might not be intentional: once you show the first page of objects again, we no longer show a `Next-Page` header, so it's not really "restarting pagination"; it's "you get the first page of results again". This might mean that you never actually see the elements that were inserted, if they were inserted in the middle.

issue_title: How to handle different Oauth providers
issue_body:
OAuth providers are giving a token that can be use to log into the service.

Today we have the Firefox Account OAuth provider which is using this formalism:

    Authorization: Bearer OAUTH-TOKEN

While implementing the Github oauth provider I've seen that [they are using TOKEN as a prefix](https://developer.github.com/v3/oauth/#use-the-access-token-to-access-the-api):

    Authorization: Token OAUTH-TOKEN

So it is easy to differentiate Firefox Account and Github because one is using TOKEN and the other one BEARER.

However, [Twitter is also using BEARER](https://dev.twitter.com/oauth/application-only) which is ambiguous because when looking at the Authorization header it is impossible to tell if it is a Firefox Account Bearer token or a Twitter Bearer token.

If we are using Bearer for both, then an application with both Firefox Account and Twitter bearer token activated will need to check the token validation on Firefox Account and then if it fails, on Twitter.

This will add complexity and will slow the authentication process while leaking Bearer tokens to another service.

My proposal is to use the Oauth service provider as the Authorization Keyword.

So that we may use this formalism when Authenticated on a Kinto service:

    Authorization: FirefoxAccount OAUTH-TOKEN
    Authorization: Twitter OAUTH-TOKEN
    Authorization: Github OAUTH-TOKEN

This will make it obvious which oauth provider to call and will not break any API.

We could also keep ``Bearer`` for Firefox Account Oauth token not to break current implementations.

I also considered adding a second header with the provider name:

    Authorization: Bearer OAUTH-TOKEN
    Provider: Twitter

But it means we need to have the context of both header to handle the Authorization Policy choice which might impact the way we are handling cache invalidation (whereas we will less likely forget about the Authorization header we might forget about handling the Provider one).

However we might want to consider this as a good solution too.

What do you think?

issue_title: Move API & CLI functions out of `pulp_smash.utils`
issue_body:
Module `pulp_smash.utils` includes the following functions that make use of the API:

* `poll_spawned_tasks`
* `poll_task`
* `reset_pulp`

It also includes a `get_broker` function that makes use of the CLI.

Let's move these functions out of `pulp_smash.utils`. Having these functions all dumped together into this single module is problematic. Most pressingly, it is impossible to move the `_get_plugin_types_ids` function into `pulp_smash.utils` due to a circular import issue!

One option is to move these functions straight to `pulp_smash.api` and `pulp_smash.cli`, respectively. This is an OK thing to do. However, it does bring up the issue of confused responsibilities: as it stands, `pulp_smash.api` and `pulp_smash.cli` are purely concerned with providing clients for talking to a server, and are not concerned with our specific needs for those clients. This is cool, b/c we could theoretically move the clients into a separate package! Here's some other options that would better promote a separation of concerns:

* We could make `pulp_smash.api` a package. We could then move the client code into `pulp_smash.api.client`, and put API utility code into `pulp_smash.api.utils`. Similar comments apply to the CLI code.
* We could add a `pulp_smash.api_utils` module. This is a lower impact option, as fewer references would need to be edited. Also, `pulp_smash.api.client.Client` is redundant, where as `pulp_smash.api.Client` is direct.

```Unhandled Exception: System.MissingMethodException: No parameterless constructor defined for this object.
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindNonScalar(IConfiguration configuration, Object instance)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindInstance(Type type, Object instance, IConfiguration config)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.Bind(IConfiguration configuration, Object instance)

   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.ConventionBasedStartup.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.EnsureApplicationServices()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.BuildApplication()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostBuilder.Build()
   at tts2.Program.Main(String[] args)
```

The exception is triggered here (only relevant code):
```c#
namespace tts2
{
    public class Program()
    {
        public static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            WebHost.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
                .UseStartup<Startup>()
                .Build();
        }
    }

    public class Startup
    {
        public IConfiguration Configuration { get; }

        public Startup(IConfiguration configuration)
        {
            Configuration = configuration;
        }

        public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
        {
            // stuff

            // The following line throws the exception when published with the IL linker
            MicrosoftLoginSettings loginSettings = new MicrosoftLoginSettings();
            Configuration.GetSection("MicrosoftLoginSettings").Bind(loginSettings);

            // stuff
        }
    }
}

namespace Tts.Settings
{
    public class MicrosoftLoginSettings
    {
        public MicrosoftLoginSettings()
        {
        }

        public string ClientId { get; set; }
        public string ClientSecret { get; set; }
    }
}
```

I added the linker step as required. My nuget.config file:
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<configuration>
 <packageSources>
  <add key="dotnet-core" value="https://dotnet.myget.org/F/dotnet-core/api/v3/index.json" />
 </packageSources>
</configuration>
```

And my tts2.csproj file:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>Tts</RootNamespace>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="ILLink.Tasks" Version="0.1.4-preview-981901" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.All" Version="2.0.0" />
    <!-- more PackageReferences... -->
  </ItemGroup>
 <!-- more ItemGroups... -->
</Project>
```


I'm guessing this has something to do with the linker not seeing through reflection, as expected. However, I decided to try the advanced options and explicitly set the assembly as root. This is what I added in my tts2.csproj:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <!-- stuff -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <LinkerRootAssemblies Include="tts2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

That didn't change the outcome, so I tried targeting the MicrosoftLoginSettings type specifically. This is what my tts2.csproj looked like then:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <!-- stuff -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <LinkerRootDescriptors Include="linker.xml" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And this is my `linker.xml` file:
```xml
<linker>
  <assembly fullname="tts2">
    <type fullname="Tts.Settings.MicrosoftLoginSettings" required="false" />
  </assembly>
</linker>
```

I also tried without the `required="false"` attribute: same result.

It seems that nothing I do prevents the linker from removing that parameterless constructor. I'm not quite sure I'm doing the right thing, so please feel free to correct me if anything above isn't right!

Clickhouse queries accept lots of settings, according to https://github.com/yandex/ClickHouse/blob/master/dbms/src/Interpreters/Settings.h#L30

If a setting is set to a value different from the default one, then it should be sent to the server.
It could be done in `connection.send_query()` before `write_binary_str('', self.fout)  # end of query settings`

Example of use:
```
c = client('server')
settings = Settings()
settings.replace_running_query = True
c.execute('SELECT 1', settings, query_id=a_query_id)
```

issue_title: use postgres naming for environment variables
issue_body:
Postgres has standard names for environment variables to specify a connection.

https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/static/libpq-envars.html

This is used by `libpq`, the command line utilities `createdb`, `psql` and even `psycopg2`. I think marabunta should use those instead of `MARABUNTA_XXX`.

Otherwise we could find ourselves typing scripts like
```bash
MARABUNTA_DB=${PGDATABASE} MARABUNTA_USER=${PGUSER} [...] marabunta
```

And so on. Also, it makes sense because those are parameters for PostgreSQL, not for marabunta.

Options in EB have support of resource_name field to aggregate group of options, so OptionSettings props should have 
```
'ResourceName': (basestring, False)
```
For example here's part of generating autoscale triggers: 
```
                  {
                        "Namespace": "aws:autoscaling:trigger",
                        "OptionName": "BreachDuration",
                        "ResourceName": "TriggerMin",
                        "Value": "5"
                    },
{
                        "Namespace": "aws:autoscaling:trigger",
                        "OptionName": "BreachDuration",
                        "ResourceName": "TriggerMax",
                        "Value": "5"
                    },
```

issue_title: StageDescription does not support all attributes
issue_body:
Support for the following attributes is missing in ApiGateway StageDescription:

```
"ThrottlingRateLimit": (positive_integer, False),
"ThrottlingBurstLimit": (positive_integer, False),
"DataTraceEnabled": (bool, False),
"MetricsEnabled": (bool, False),
"LoggingLevel": (basestring, False),
"CachingEnabled": (bool, False),
```

I suggest we have a convenient method allowing to invoke `ctest` and integrate it within existing test runners like `pytest`, `nose` or `unittest`.

That way, we wouldn't have to directly invoke ctest like it is done [here](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/pull/38/files#r68153673) 

Instead `setup.py test` could be done.

For reference: Similarly to what is done in [pytest](https://pytest.org/latest/goodpractices.html#integrating-with-setuptools-python-setup-py-test-pytest-runner),
Source distribution in PyPI does not have the cmake files because of this

issue_title: Command 'raven test' returns 0 when 'Unsupported protocol version' occure
issue_body:
***What is happening?***

```shell
$ bin/raven test http:/123:abc@sentry.domain.com/2
Using DSN configuration:
  http://123:abc@sentry.domain.com/2

Client configuration:
  servers        : ['http://sentry.domain.com/api/2/store/']
  project        : 2
  public_key     : 123
  secret_key     : abc

Sending a test message... success!
Event ID was '1234abcd'
Unable to capture event: APIError(Client/server version mismatch: Unsupported protocol version (6))
Failed to submit message: u'This is a test message generated using ``raven test``'

$ echo $?
0
```

***What should happen?***
Raven should return non-zero exit code.

issue_title: Unit training seems improperly split across barracks
issue_body:
As an example, when attempting to train marines out of 3 barracks, if one of the barracks has a techlab, it seems all commands to train are queued in that barracks first, before commands are split across the other barracks:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/998458/40265820-f85e273c-5b82-11e8-8f76-645b56c9f13f.png)

When all 3 barracks have no tech lab the training is spread more evenly across all 3.

## Reasons for dropping

Python 3.3 went [EOL last month](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CPython#Version_history) and is no longer receiving security updates.

* Here's the pip installs for SoCo from PyPI for the last month,  showing low numbers for 3.3:
```
$ pypinfo --percent --pip soco pyversion
python_version percent download_count
-------------- ------- --------------
3.5              37.2%            823
3.6              29.0%            641
2.7              17.0%            377
3.4              15.3%            338
3.3               1.5%             33
2.6               0.0%              1
```

* pip 10 will deprecate Python 3.3 support, pip 11 won't support it (https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3796)
* Very little PyPI traffic (June 2016) https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3796
* Allows the introduction of non-3.3 code without the concern of 3.3 failing
* Frees up resources on Travis CI for SoCo and everyone else

## Reasons for keeping

* Supporting 3.3 in addition to 2.7/3.4+ isn't as difficult as supporting 2.6 or 3.2

---

Anything else, for or against?

An alternative to dropping is deprecating: announcing it will be removed in a future version.

Permission denied error occurs on 'manage.py collectstatic' execution
[-shell=True] (https://github.com/jazzband/django-pipeline/commit/9be0f2934667d0fc199fac30e6f9a0e7115888ec#diff-fde7a3bb47631ce4a4007fb9d5b36927L246)

### Describe the problem

Is it possible to override Config meta class and change 'Config' to 'My text'?
There is a link to my stackoverflow question: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44914601/django-change-app-name-in-admin

### System configuration

* Django version: 1.10.5
* Python version: 3.5.2

issue_title: Building UI during setup.py sometimes fails during development.
issue_body:
If I find myself running `python setup.py develop` after `rm -rf python/ray/core` then it will fail.

If I comment out the call to `build_ui.sh` in `build_thirdparty.sh` then I get the error

```
error: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'ray/core/src/catapult_files/index.html'
```

These issues make development less convenient.

### Describe the problem

Currently the lambda syntax is overloaded to mean "sample a value from this function". This makes it impossible to specify an actual lambda function.

### System information
- **Linux Ubuntu 16.04**:
- **Ray installed from binary**:
- **0.4**:
- **3.6.5**:

Thank you for developing this cool library. 

At the version 0.4, `ray.tune` seems to depend on `cv2` and `gym` etc but they are not explicit dependencies. I think they are unnecessary for `ray.tune` so is it possible to remove these dependencies? Temporarily, I'm using version 0.3.1 instead.
The PyPI package (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/django-cache-utils) seems to be quite old. Please update it to current version.

issue_title: Update event if already exists is not possible any more with add_event

issue_title: Build issue: PyWavelets does not install from sdist
issue_body:
PyWavelets fails to install from sdist with standard Python tooling because it relies on presence of *numpy* in its *setup.py*. Thus, running `pip install --no-binary :all: ...` or `python setup.py sdist` fails with a clean Python installation (no external packages). Since the package is published on PyPI (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/PyWavelets), it needs to be able to build from source distribution (*tar.gz* file) self-sufficiently.

The fact that the package imports *numpy* in *setup.py* indicates that the package needs *numpy* in `setup_requires`. This creates a chicken-and-egg kind of problem that is not easy to hack around, but it may be possible. Please take a look at *scipy* and what they are doing in *setup.py*: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/master/setup.py. This may help with fixing this issue for PyWavelets.

Are there any plans for implementing causal Bayesian networks?

DJA 2.3.0 supports 1.8 - 1.11. At the end of the month, Django 1.10 will no longer be supported by the Django core team and the 1.8 LTS support is ending early next year. issue_title: Use custom class loader for autoclass
issue_body:
We are currently trying to use groovy grape to load classes from jars that were not on the initial class path to allow for a more interactive scripting experience. We can resolve and download the required jars but we cannot load the classes through `autoclass`. It seems the issue is with [FindClass](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/jni/spec/functions.html#FindClass):
>Since Java 2 SDK release 1.2, when FindClass is called through the Invocation Interface, there is no current native method or its associated class loader. In that case, the result of ClassLoader.getSystemClassLoader is used. This is the class loader the virtual machine creates for applications, and is able to locate classes listed in the java.class.path property.

We tried replicating `autoclass` to accept a class object that was created like this:
```python
loaded_class = java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(clazz)
autoclass(loaded_class)
print(loaded_class)
```
```
<Class at 0x7f9ab7a70468 jclass=java/lang/Class jself=<LocalRef obj=0x106e178 at 0x7f9ab7a863f0>>
```
I do not know how to replicate
[reflect.py:159](https://github.com/kivy/pyjnius/blob/master/jnius/reflect.py#L159):
More specifically, I cannot replicate [jnius_export_func.pxi:30-33](https://github.com/kivy/pyjnius/blob/master/jnius/jnius_export_func.pxi#L30-L33)
```python
cls = Class(noinstance=True)
cls.instanciate_from(create_local_ref(j_env, jc))
j_env[0].DeleteLocalRef(j_env, jc)
return cls
```
for `loaded_class` as created through a custom class loader as above.

issue_title: support assets like MXNet model server or tensorflow savedmodel
issue_body:
I read the IR spec documents, did not find any field to represent assets like tensorflow savedModel format or MXNet model server, but that is necessary when we have vocabularies, etc.

tensorflow savedModel
```
Assets
    Subfolder called assets.
    Contains auxiliary files such as vocabularies, etc.
Extra assets
    Subfolder where higher-level libraries and users can add their own assets that co-exist with the model, but are not loaded by the graph.
    This subfolder is not managed by the SavedModel libraries.
```

MXNet model server:
```
Model Definition (json file) - contains the layers and overall structure of the neural network
Model Params and Weights (params file) - contains the parameters and the weights
Model Signature (json file) - defines the inputs and outputs that MMS is expecting to hand-off to the API
assets (text files) - auxiliary files that support model inference such as vocabularies, labels, etc. and vary depending on the model
```

This section is not read:
```

[table.hosts_critical_threshold]
type=integer
title=Hosts critical threshold
visible=True

[table.hosts_warning_threshold]
type=integer
title=Hosts warning threshold
visible=True

```

issue_title: Unable to dynamically add attributes to Document objects
issue_body:
Due to `__slots__` being defined on `docx.document.Document`, attributes cannot be dynamically added to documents.

I wish to create a method that returns a document object pre-formatted with some tables that the caller will later modify, I would like to reference these tables as attributes on the document so they can be accessed by name.

Can `__slots__` be removed from `docx.document.Document`?

issue_title: registration of NotFound view in cornice 0.6 does not work
issue_body: When registering the NotFound view, webob.exc.HTTNotFound is used as context. This will not work as expected. Using pyramid.httpexceptions.HTTPNotFound as context does give the expected result.

issue_title: Another `IndexError: list index out of range`
issue_body:
**Version** `mythril==0.13.9`

Perhaps related to #25, contract [`0xE0F7e56e61b4267062172494D7506087205A4229`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xe0f7e56e61b4267062172494d7506087205a4229) fails to parse. Etherscan shows there are 89,000 [other contracts](https://etherscan.io/find-similiar-contracts?a=0xe0f7e56e61b4267062172494d7506087205a4229&lvl=5) with this matching bytecode, a large fraction of which were created at the same time?

``` bash
>> myth --rpc localhost:8545 -x -a 0xE0F7e56e61b4267062172494D7506087205A4229

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/myth", line 406, in <module>
    issues = fire_lasers(sym)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/mythril/analysis/security.py", line 19, in fire_lasers
    issues += module.execute(statespace)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/mythril/analysis/modules/unchecked_retval.py", line 53, in execute
    _state = call.node.states[call.state_index + i]
IndexError: list index out of range
```

I keep getting this error when i try validate the signatures (Step3 on https://gethttpsfree.com)

Error: Account registration failed. Please start back at Step 1. { "type": "urn:acme:error:badNonce", "detail": "JWS has invalid anti-replay nonce Hjmjx7usi9Eo_hroR1rStJgDDIdONvBbkFc2HI85SdA", "status": 400 }

Once #1016 is done, it would need interactive UI if it is going to work for the maximum number of users.  It should call out to IDisplay once for each domain name, to figure out what the webroot is.  Ideally if the domains are `example.com` and `www.example.com` the text box for the second of them can be pre-populated with the preceding value?

The current documentation for the [Python acme client](http://letsencrypt.readthedocs.io/projects/acme/en/latest/index.html) is fairly lacking. The example client doesn't demonstrate how to set up challenges, and the docs for [`acme.challenges`](http://letsencrypt.readthedocs.io/projects/acme/en/latest/api/challenges.html) also does not give an example on how to set up challenges, nor does it provide enough description on how each class should be used.

This makes it difficult for someone to use the Python acme client to mint their own certs without either having to dig through the code for letsencrypt (certbot) or shelling out to certbot itself.

Compare this with the example client in for the [Ruby acme client](https://github.com/unixcharles/acme-client/blob/master/README.md), which gives a full working client and an (arguably) easier to use API.
### TLDR
There should be a full end-to-end example for the Python acme client to make it easier to learn.

After talking to the maintainers we're going to make https://github.com/lifeonmarspt/certbot-route53 an official plugin and move it into our repo. Things we want with this transition are:

* Git history is preserved
* We have a plugin under the name certbot-dns-route53 in our tree
* We have a plugin or at least something to upload to the name certbot-route53 on PyPI so users under that name continue to get updates as the plugin is further developed. In theory, this could be the same package as `certbot-dns-route53` but we'd want to make sure the user experience is sane if both are installed.
* The certbot-route53 package should continue to work with the plugin name `certbot-route53:auth` due to the way we name 3rd party plugins (see #4351). Whether or not it also works with `certbot-route53` is an implementation detail in my opinion.

I don't really have a preference for how this is implemented. My instinct is it'll be easiest to make `certbot-route53` a shim package around `certbot-dns-route53`. We did something similar for the `letsencrypt` package in our 0.7.0 release. This can be left up to whoever takes this ticket though.

@zjs, if you have an interest in working on this, that'd be useful, otherwise, I can take it when I get some spare cycles.

issue_title: Provide example of RieszRotationMatrix applied to RieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator
issue_body:
[RieszRotationMatrix](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKIsotropicWavelets/blob/master/include/itkRieszRotationMatrix.h), as already documented, provides a way to transform an input [physical rotation matrix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rotation_matrix) into a matrix that can be used to steer riesz components generated from the [RieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKIsotropicWavelets/blob/master/include/itkRieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator.h).

Please note that the output of the RieszGenerator is a std::vector of output images.
The RieszRotationMatrix is a itkVariableSizeMatrix, so you can get the resulting matrix with rieszRotationMatrix.GetVnlMatrix().

The problem raised by @PierreFontaine is that we do not provide any method to apply the RieszRotationMatrix to the output of the RieszGenerator.

A new method should be provided to do this.

issue_body:
This should've been obvious from the beginning (given offlineimap's development), but I guess it's never too late: We should switch to sqlite for storing the status metadata in `<statusfolder>/<pair>/<collection>.items`.

* `.collections` files should still be JSON, only the format for `.items` changes. At least I see no reason to do otherwise.
* The codebase for loading and saving `.collections` and `.items` files is weirdly entangled. We need to fix that.
* `sync` should take subclass of a `Status` interface. Requirement to remove coupling to either in-memory dict or sqlite as status storage, because otherwise we can't write decent unit tests. 

Goal: Ability to modify status in-place (instead of having "load" and "save" steps) opens the door to optimizing memory-efficiency of sync. I played with this once and it's not easy. We'll see.

issue_title: Actually remove content when it's no longer referenced
issue_body: When content is no longer referenced by any local content DB, it should be removed from disk so the user can make room for other content.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/30356735-84bdbccc-97ef-11e7-8022-c05fdaab95ea.png)

We use this material icon for channels:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/30356759-b11de99a-97ef-11e7-86ee-241776fef347.png)

### Observed behavior

A couple of places, API calls will return HTTP `400`

>  A 400 means that the request was malformed. In other words, the data stream sent by the client to the server didn't follow the rules. 

If the username already exists (see: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/3125 ) then the HTTP status code 400 is used as the sole condition.

Another example:

https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/blob/79c6a9046b153656c61c01b9375109d3ff395ae5/kolibri/plugins/user/assets/src/views/sign-up-page/index.vue#L192

I don't think it's that bad, but I don't think we should assume anything from the HTTP status code. Some people even argue that `200` is more correct for a form processing where data doesn't validate.. because frankly, there's no exception taking place in the server, and the client is using the API correctly.

Let's find and add a standard for form validation errors? cc: @mrpau-eugene @indirectlylit @rtibbles 

### Expected behavior

Backend errors should resolve through correct error codes that do not mis-guide developers.

### User-facing consequences

None

### Errors and logs

n/a

### Steps to reproduce

n/a

### Context

n/a

### Observed behavior

Current nav:

* Recommended (default)
* Channels
* Classes (visible if enrolled)

### New behavior

* Classes (visible and default if enrolled)
* Channels (default if not enrolled)
* Recommended

### User-facing consequences

Most salient and helpful tabs should be most prominent



### Context

0.8
See jquery/jquery#3609. To be removed in 4.0.

issue_title: Cryptic Error Message about "Watchman crawl failed"
issue_body:
It seems I installed watchman via brew back in April 2005 when I was experimenting with React-Native.  Since then I have not used watchman and completely forgot it was even installed.

That being said, this morning I was running Jest and I received this error.

```
jest-haste-map: Watchman crawl failed. Retrying once with node crawler. 
Usually this happens when watchman isn't running. Create an empty `.watchmanconfig` file in your project's root folder or initialize a git or hg repository in your project.
Error: Watchman error: unknown command watch-project. Make sure watchman is running for this project. See https://facebook.github.io/watchman/docs/troubleshooting.html.
```

I have the same repo on another machine without watchman installed and obviously did not receive the error.

It took some digging to figure out what the error was for, if it was Jest related or that I even had watchman installed. 

I think it may be helpful in the error message to give a little more context. I am not sure if this is something that should be handled by the Jest team or Watchman team. 

I just thought I would offer my two cents.

Thanks.

issue_title: Potential false error with path insensitive check
issue_body:
On linux, I invoke a program that uses watchman with a path like `my-program ./foo`.

By default, how the shell/`my-program` end up passing this to watchman is as `/home/stephen/./foo`. E.g. note the the "noop" `.`.

After upgrading to watchman 4.9.0, it does not like this:

```
watchman error: unable to resolve root /home/stephen/./local-repo: "/home/stephen/./local-repo" resolved to "/home/stephen/local-repo" but we were unable to examine "/home/stephen/./local-repo" using strict case sensitive rules.
```

I'm writing Java, so if I do `path.toFile().getCanonicalFile().toPath()` this strips the noop `./` so path becomes just `/home/stephen/local-repo` and then it works great, so I'm not blocked, but thought I'd file the bug as seems like in theory watchman should not complain about this path, as AFAIU there is no case issues with it.

issue_title: Watchman seems unusable on OS X
issue_body:
Creating a new issue becaus the existing ones don't seem to fit the bill entirely.

I installed nuclide on Atom 1.6 on OS X 10.11.4, and installed watchman 4.5.0 (via [Nix](http://nixos.org/nix/), [binaries](http://hydra.nixos.org/build/32398307) - [build log](http://hydra.nixos.org/build/33715218/log)). 

I have a few React projects with a fair amount of files in them (190780 in total across the dirs in the watchman watch list), and when I just use Atom like I did before installing Nuclide, my system starts to misbehave because the kernel file tables are full (necessitating restart).

I had to set `kern.maxfiles` to 30000 via sysctl just to be able to use the system normally, and even then watchman skips huge sets of my files with `[io <project dir>] opendir(<project subdir>/) -> Too many open files. Marking this portion of the tree deleted`. Instant search in Atom doesn't find all files.

So it seems to me that watchman is not doing the right thing when monitoring files on OS X, given that Atom without Nuclide works fine…
